title: J&K: Massive forest fire breaks out in Rajouri amid extreme heatwave - Asianet Newsable
search_term: heatwave (Anantnag OR Bandipore OR Baramulla OR Budgam OR Doda OR Ganderbal OR Jammu OR Kathua OR Kishtwar OR Kulgam OR Kupwara OR Poonch OR Pulwama OR Rajouri OR Ramban OR Reasi OR Samba OR Shopian OR Srinagar OR Udhampur) Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
A massive forest fire has erupted across multiple locations in the Rajouri Forest Division of Jammu and Kashmir, fueled by an ongoing extreme heatwave. Various departments and local volunteers are battling the blaze to prevent further spread.
Due to the country's ongoing extreme heatwave, a large forest fire has started at several locations throughout the Rajouri Forest Division. The teams from the Forest Department, Forest Protection Force, Social Forestry Department, and local volunteers have been deployed in extensive firefighting operations to halt the advance of the blaze and prevent it from spreading into dense forest regions.
Heatwave-Fuelled Blaze Spreads Across Rajouri
Divisional Forest Officer (DFO) Rajouri, Naveed Iqbal, stated that the blaze has spread across multiple areas of the division. "We have personally visited two locations; this is our third. Furthermore, reports have come in from one or two other locations where we have already deployed our teams. We hope to successfully control the fire and minimise the damage," Iqbal told ANI.
Iqbal stated that rising temperatures and dry weather conditions are believed to be the primary reasons behind the sudden outbreak of fires across multiple locations. "Rising temperatures and a dry spell are leading to many reported fire incidents. Whenever the department receives information about such an event, we immediately deploy staff. They work alongside residents to control the fire and minimise any potential damage," said Iqbal.
Authorities Appeal for Public Vigilance
Hence, authorities have appealed to the public to avoid activities that could trigger forest fires. Iqbal further requested the public to immediately report any smoke or fire incidents to nearby officials. "I appeal to the public to avoid lighting fires even in private areas during this dry spell, as these can become uncontrollable and spread to forests, causing immense loss. We are investigating the causes of these fires. While many are due to negligence, there may also be deliberate attempts by mischievous elements. I want to emphasise that legal action will be taken against anyone who deliberately sets a fire in the forest. I urge the public to remain vigilant and report any instances of people starting fires so that appropriate legal measures can be taken," added Iqbal. (ANI)
(Except for the headline, this story has not been edited by Asianet Newsable English staff and is published from a syndicated feed.)
